items.title: Achieving net-zero carbon emissions can reduce the intensity, duration, and frequency of heat waves
items.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Dr. Chloe Brimacombe. She presents a clear and sobering analysis of the current European heatwave as both a meteorological event and a societal stress test. Her argument is that the exceptional temperatures affecting France are not merely unusual because of their intensity, but because they combine several compounding factors: their early arrival in the season, elevated humidity that amplifies health risks, and a broader trend toward longer and more frequent heatwaves across Europe. While she emphasizes the continued necessity of decarbonization and achieving net-zero emissions, her analysis focuses equally on adaptation. Schools, hospitals, care homes, and public infrastructure must be redesigned for a hotter climate.

items.title: 'A very different Afghanistan: Violence now linked to socioeconomic pressures'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Silvia Boccardi, freelance journalist based in Rome. Nearly five years after the Taliban's return to power, Afghanistan occupies an uneasy position in international politics. As European governments increasingly seek practical engagement with the authorities in Kabul, questions persist over whether diplomatic contact can alleviate humanitarian suffering without conferring legitimacy upon a regime whose restrictions on women and civil liberties remain widely condemned.

items.description: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

items.title: Wang Yi calls for India, China to accelerate resumption of stalled dialogue mechanisms
items.description: A lengthier Chinese readout of the talks between NSA Ajit Doval and China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi said that the Chinese leader noted that “India is an important neighbour of China”

items.title: GameStop CEO Cohen spurns $35 billion pay plan to focus on plan to buy eBay
School offers migrant children alternative to irregular migration
items.description: Established by a local organization supporting migrants and refugees, the educational center provides lessons in French, Arabic, and mathematics to children who are often living in highly vulnerable conditions.

items.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
items.description: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

items.title: Live: Minister says Australian housing market experiencing a correction
items.description: Housing Minister Clare O'Neil says Australia's housing market is experiencing a correction following COVID amid low auction clearance rates in major capital cities. Follow live.

items.title: Maradona was 'shielded' by people around him before death, trial hears
items.description: Witness echoed testimony from previous witnesses, including relatives, doctors and healthcare workers, who have described the house where Maradona was looked after as dirty and lacking the necessary medical equipment. Leer más

items.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
items.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

items.title: U.S. and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
items.description: A joint statement from mediating nations ​Qatar and Pakistan said the U.S. and Iran agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days

items.title: Lebanon's lose-lose: Is there another way out of conflict?
items.description: The new US-Iran peace deal seems to be forcing Lebanon to choose between Israel's sphere of influence or Iran's. It wants neither. But could there be a better alternative, achieved with the help of regional diplomacy?

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Top UN official visits refugee inclusion project in Ethiopia
items.description: The UN High Commissioner for Refugees, Barham Salih, decribed the Makatet programme as an “important ambitious roadmap”.

items.title: England - Ghana: The Three Lions push hard but the Black Stars hold firm
items.description: England and Ghana cancelled each other out in a goalless draw at Foxborough Stadium, a result that leaves Group L finely poised with all four nations still harbouring knockout qualification hopes. Relive our live coverage.

items.title: Natural Resources Governance Institute seeks stronger methane rules enforcement in Niger Delta
items.description: “Companies should not only disclose emissions, but actively manage and reduce them.” The post Natural Resources Governance Institute seeks stronger methane rules enforcement in Niger Delta appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Netanyahu says Israel should 'break free' of U.S. military aid
items.description: "I deeply appreciate the support we have received from our American friends, but we need to break free from dependency and build our own independent armaments network," the premier told reserve officers on a training course in the occupied West Bank

items.title: Sri Lanka to appoint military-led dengue monitoring unit amid surge in cases
items.description: Health authorities have reported a total of 46,037 cases across the island, as of June 20

items.title: World Cup exploits of Maradona and Messi have Argentina fans in raptures
items.description: Fans buzzing after Lionel Messi bags another two goals in Argentina's second World Cup 2026 match. Leer más

items.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
items.description: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful. DW has more.

items.title: Few in U.S. say Iran war was worth it; Trump approval ties lowest of term, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
items.description: Only 23% of Americans — including just half of Republicans — think the U.S. is now in a stronger position with ‌Iran compared with its position before the war

items.title: Burkina Faso: Rights groups urge release of disappeared journalist
items.description: It has been two years since Serge Oulon, an investigative journalist and editor was taken from his home in Ougadougou, Burkina Faso's capital, by armed men claiming to be state agents

items.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3
items.description: An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
items.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

items.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
items.description: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

items.title: Trump, without proof, blames ‘vandalism’ as Reflecting Pool turns green again
items.description: With algae-green water and blue peeling paint, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is set to be drained again, despite Donald Trump's recent renovation costing over $14 million. Trump has blamed the problems on "vandalism," claiming a 350-foot gash was deliberately cut into the pool and saying multiple arrests have been made. However, he has not publicly provided evidence for the claims, despite journalists pressing him for proof.

items.title: Why do the UK’s prime ministers keep resigning?
items.description: Keir Starmer is the latest to step down, after less than two years in office.

items.title: World Cup: Iran plays 10-man Belgium to 0-0 draw
items.description: Team Melli's dream of advancing to a World Cup knockout round for the first time is still very much alive, thanks to an afternoon of brilliance from goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand in a 0-0 draw with Belgium on Sunday

items.title: Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?
items.description: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

items.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
items.description: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

items.title: Air conditioning for all? France divided over response to record-breaking heatwave
items.description: The heat is on. Enough to make Parisians take the plunge into the once notoriously polluted Canal Saint Martin. France this Tuesday awakening from its hottest night on record, enough to test the tempers of city dwellers as they sizzle under the zinc roofs of top floor apartments built for a bygone age. And while an unprecedented heat dome stalls over western Europe for God knows how long.

items.title: U.S. Senate for the first time approves War Powers resolution in rebuke to Trump over Iran conflict
items.description: It was the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50-48, was a stunning turnaround from past efforts

items.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
items.description: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

items.title: Armed group in Nigeria kills at least 20 people; exchanges gunfire with police
items.description: “The attack occurred in the Kawel community located in the Bokkos area of Plateau State,” police spokesperson said in a statement

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: US Commerce Secretary signals possible action on Chinese robots after review, Politico reports
Starmer vows ‘orderly’ transition as Labour MPs mull bid to be PM
items.description: The PM, in office for almost two years, told his senior Ministerial team during their weekly meeting that he wanted an "orderly" handover and whoever replaces him "to succeed"

items.title: Manuel Adorni asset declaration shows wealth grew 45-fold in 2024
items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más

items.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
items.description: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

items.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
items.description: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

items.title: Kenya labour minister accused over Russian forced recruitment
items.description: A Kenyan family's search for opportunity has ended in tragedy and sparked serious allegations against Labour Minister Alfred Mutua.

items.title: François Englert of Higgs boson fame passes away at 93
items.description: Englert is best known for his work in the early 1960s about how subatomic particles get their property of mass, for which he won the 2013 physics Nobel Prize alongside Peter Higgs

items.title: Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling
items.description: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

items.title: UN starts evacuating 11,000 stranded sailors from Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Following the start of the US-Israel war on Iran on February 28, Tehran had effectively closed off the vital waterway.

items.title: Ethiopian Prime Minister's party sweeps parliamentary vote
items.description: Abiy Ahmed's Prosperity Party (PP) won a landslide victory in legislative polls held June 1, the election commission said Sunday, giving it almost 90 percent of the seats

items.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees
items.description: US top diplomat Rubio said Washington will not accept tolls on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran has said they want to charge "maritime service fees." And Israel and Lebanon are set for talks in the US.

items.title: Google's YouTube settles with plaintiff ahead of second California trial over social media harm to children, attorneys say
US Supreme Court backs Trump policy on green card holder rights
items.description: Court rules 6-3 that suspicion alone justifies placing green card holders on immigration parole at border crossings.

items.title: Trump ally wins Colombia election, heralding pro-market turn
items.description: Election of conservative lawyer heralds swing back to business-friendly and pro-US policies after four years of leftist rule under Gustavo Petro. Leer más

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: German fans celebrate close win over Ivory Coast
items.description: The Elephants are, however, still in with a chance to make it to the next round for the first time in their history.
